title: KDE plasma-keyboard text input not captured
author: NarkyAbyss
body: ## Environment - **OS:** opensuse w/ KDE wayland - **Scrcpy version:** 4.0 - **Installation method:** opensuse repo - **Device model:** oneplus Nord - **Android version:** 12L ## Describe the bug new KDE plasma-keyboard update added a feature to add accented characters on any type of keyboard. while useful, the implementation seems to capture key input before it reaches scrcpy. I have verified this affects all keyboard modes: `uhid` `aoa` and `sdk` I am not sure scrcpy can do anything about this but I thought a good idea to document the issue for others. ## Workaround Disable the feature in your keyboard settings

title: Flag to ignore unknown/ambiguous arguments
author: e-t-l
body:
- [X] I have checked that a similar [feature request](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22feature+request%22) does not already exist.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
If I make a typo in an argument or pass a longer array of arguments to Scrcpy from a batch script, if an argument is `unknown option` or `ambiguous option`, then scrcpy will show that error message ***and fail to launch.***

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add an optional parameter such as `--ignore-bad-args` so that even if Scrcpy receives one unknown/ambiguous argument, it will still launch.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Writing an argument-validation script to intercept Scrcpy calls from other scripts and validate parameter names against a dynamic list of parameters accepted by the current version of Scrcpy. Not simple.

**Additional context**
n/a

title: Desktop mode not working on pixel 10 pro with --new-display
author: LiskoFINAL
body:
## Environment

 - **OS:** macOS
 - **Scrcpy version:** 4.0
 - **Installation method:** brew
 - **Device model:** Google Pixel 10 PRO
 - **Android version:** 17

## Describe the bug

Normal mirroring works fine but when I start scrcpy with --new-display to get the desktop mode, I get a solid background and the dock with the app launcher but trying to open any app doesn't do anything. I can open something by long-pressing an app icon and using a shortcut but it opens in full screen with no windows or borders or controls. I don't know if it's just a device bug\limitation. I connect thru wireless adb if could matter. Would be nice to get a feedback from another pixel 10 user as well.

title: Cannot paste text from PC to device on HONOR (Android 16), no error logs
author: ForeveR2326
body:
Device-to-PC clipboard synchronization works perfectly, but PC-to-device fails completely without throwing any errors in the console.
Copy on phone, paste on PC: Works as expected. The console successfully outputs INFO: Device clipboard copied.
Copy on PC, paste on phone: Fails. The text is not synchronized to the phone's clipboard, and no additional logs or error messages appear in the terminal.

PS D:\Program Files\scrcpy-win64-v4.0> .\scrcpy

title: scrcpy always errors out with "server connection failed" and then adb does something weird
author: opsonc
body:
## Environment

 - **OS:** Windows 11 25H2 (26200.8655)
 - **Scrcpy version:** 4.0 (behavior also met in version 3.3.4)
 - **Installation method:** Windows release
 - **Device model:** Samsung Galaxy Z Flip3 5G (although this happens on every device I try using)
 - **Android version:** 15 (but happens on lower/higher versions too)

## Describe the bug

On launch, scrcpy pushes "scrcpy-server" to device, then says "error: Server connection failed", exits, then adb starts being weird, resulting in the device reconnecting to the PC, and I have to press enter in the Command Prompt in order for me to continue using it

title: scrcpy 4.0 -- keyboard not working properly
author: huang-jy
body:
_Please read the [prerequisites] to run scrcpy._

[prerequisites]: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy#prerequisites

_Also read the [FAQ] and check if your [issue][issues] already exists._

[FAQ]: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md
[issues]: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/issues

## Environment

 - **OS:** Fedora
 - **Scrcpy version:** 4.0
 - **Installation method:** dnf
 - **Device model:** OnePlus 9 Pro
 - **Android version:** 14

## Describe the bug

When using scrcpy 4.0, typing does not work properly, some keys work, such as punctuations marks, semi colon, colon, etc. but normal letters do not work

Killing adb via `adb kill-server` then running Vysor (an alternative tool) has working typing, so I know it's not something related to hardware

I also verified this by extracting scrcpy v4.0 from the releases page and running off there -- same problem.

However, when I tried extracting and using the previous version (v3.3.4) via the releases page, typing works fine. So something must have been introduced in the v4.0 that is breaking or causing problems with the typing

title: The latest "Android SDK Platform-Tools 37.0.0": Incompatible with Windows Versions < 10
author: diassynthesis
body:
## Environment

 - **OS:** Windows 7]
 - **Scrcpy version:** [4.0]
 - **Installation method:** [ Windows release 4.0: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/releases/download/v4.0/scrcpy-win64-v4.0.zip]
 - **Device model:* redmi Note 8 Pro*
 - **Android version:** [e.g. 11]

## Describe the bug

Error when launching the "scrcpy.exe" due to the execution of the new "adb":                                                                  <<<Signature du problĂ¨meÂ :
  Nom dâ€™Ă©vĂ©nement de problĂ¨me:	BEX
  Nom de lâ€™application:	adb.exe
  Version de lâ€™application:	0.0.0.0
  Horodatage de lâ€™application:	0bf586cc
  Nom du module par dĂ©faut:	kernel32.dll_unloaded
  Version du module par dĂ©faut:	0.0.0.0
  Horodateur du module par dĂ©faut:	5c6e2225
  DĂ©calage de lâ€™exception:	75d94791
  Code de lâ€™exception:	c0000005
  DonnĂ©es dâ€™exception:	00000008
  Version du systĂ¨me:	6.1.7601.2.1.0.256.1
  Identificateur de paramĂ¨tres rĂ©gionaux:	1036
  Information supplĂ©mentaire nÂ°Â 1:	4c0d
  Information supplĂ©mentaire nÂ°Â 2:	4c0d4d78887f76d971d5d00f1f20a433
  Information supplĂ©mentaire nÂ°Â 3:	d28d
  Information supplĂ©mentaire nÂ°Â 4:	d28d2f24b64a34f81af4f919f5f9fcf4
>>>                                                   
```
Terminal output:

G:\scrcpy-win64-v4.0>scrcpy.exe -v
scrcpy 4.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>

Dependencies (compiled / linked):
 - SDL: 3.4.8 / 3.4.8
 - libavcodec: 62.28.101 / 62.28.101
 - libavformat: 62.12.101 / 62.12.101
 - libavutil: 60.26.101 / 60.26.101
 - libusb: - / 1.0.29

G:\scrcpy-win64-v4.0>adb --version

G:\scrcpy-win64-v4.0>scrcpy.exe --pause-on-exit=if-error
scrcpy 4.0 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
ERROR: "adb start-server" returned with value 3221225477
ERROR: Could not start adb server
ERROR: Server connection failed
Press Enter to continue...
```

NB: Android SDK Platform-Tools 37.0.0 is not compatible with Windows 7. Modern versions of Google's platform tools, including adb.exe and fastboot.exe, rely heavily on Universal C Runtime (UCRT) function calls and Windows API sets (such as api-ms-win-core-...) that are natively missing or completely unsupported on older operating systems

The solution that works for me: Using "https://github.com/YuZhouRen86/VxKex-NEXT" for "adb.exe".

Please do not post screenshots of your terminal, just post the content as text instead.

title: Camera configuration error / Demuxer error on Xiaomi POCO (Android 13) since v4.0
author: ReynoTD
body: ### Describe the bug In scrcpy v4.0, using the device camera (`--video-source=camera`) fails with `Camera configuration error` and `Demuxer error` on my Xiaomi POCO unless `--camera-ar=16:9` is specified. However, when forcing 16:9, it opens a massive 4K texture (`3840x2160`), causing high instability, encoding lag, and frequent disconnections (`Camera disconnected`). Any attempt to limit the resolution using `--max-size` or `--camera-size` results in an immediate crash (`Camera configuration error`). **This worked perfectly in v3.3.4**, where the camera successfully initialized and downscaled to `1920x1080` without any errors or stability issues. ### Environment * **scrcpy version:** 4.0 (broken) / 3.3.4 (working) * **Operating system:** Windows 11 (using Direct3D11 renderer) * **Device model:** Xiaomi POCO 2207117BPG * **Android version:** 13 ### Camera List Output (`scrcpy --list-cameras`) ```text --camera-id=0 (back, 4624x3472, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 10]) --camera-id=1 (front, 4208x3120, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 4]) --camera-id=2 (back, 3280x2464, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 4]) --camera-id=3 (back, 1600x1200, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 4]) --camera-id=4 (back, 1600x1200, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 10]) --camera-id=5 (back, 4624x3472, fps={10, 15, 20, 30}, zoom-range=[1, 4]) ``` ### Commands tested and their behaviors in v4.0: 1. **Immediate Crash (Strict Size):** ```scrcpy --video-source=camera --camera-id=0 --camera-size=1920x1080 --camera-fps=30 -b 10M --no-audio``` 2. **Delayed Crash / Disconnection (Scaled Size):** ```scrcpy --video-source=camera --camera-id=0 --max-size=1920 --camera-fps=30 -b 10M --no-audio``` Result: It successfully calculates the 4:3 downscale to 1920x1440, but the camera immediately disconnects after a few frames 3. **Unstable & Blurry (Forced 16:9 Aspect Ratio):** ```scrcpy --video-source=camera --camera-id=0 --camera-ar=16:9 --camera-fps=30 -b 10M --no-audio``` Result: Opens a massive 4K texture (3840x2160), which causes severe encoding lag, blurriness, and eventual disconnection. --- ### Evidence from v3.3.4 (Working perfectly) ```scrcpy --video-source=camera --camera-id=0 --camera-size=1920x1080 --camera-fps=30 -b 10M --no-audio```

title: I can't authorize device
author: ibxsv
body:
I am unable to authorize USB Debugging on my Samsung Galaxy A52s 5G (Android 14, One ui 6.1) with a broken display. The device is detected by adb but always shows as unauthorized. 

Usb debugging is definitely enabled, and i can unlock the phone with my fingerprint.
The steps i'm doing are as followed:
plug in the phone to the pc with an usb cable, i type scrcpy into the cmd, it shows this
ERROR: Device is unauthorized:
ERROR:     -->   (usb)  R5CT12JYPRL               unauthorized
ERROR: A popup should open on the device to request authorization.
ERROR: Check the FAQ: <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/master/FAQ.md>
ERROR: Server connection failed

i type adb devices, it shows this
List of devices attached
R5CT12JYPRL     unauthorized

i type in adb kill-server and right after that adb start-server, it shows this
* daemon not running; starting now at tcp:5037
* daemon started successfully

and then finally i type in scrcpy --otg, and try doing the combination which i found online, tab enter tab tab enter, after doing that i close the otg and try scrcpy again, but it still shows that it's unauthorized.

I don't know what to do.
